# ApexCSS CLI — LLM Reference

> Machine-readable reference for the apexcss-cli package (v0.4.3).
> Follows the llms.txt convention: https://llmstxt.org

---

## Project

- **Name:** apexcss-cli
- **Version:** 0.4.3
- **Description:** CLI tool for building, optimising, and managing the ApexCSS utility-first CSS framework.
- **Language:** TypeScript (ESM, Node.js ≥ 22)
- **Repository:** https://github.com/chris-briddock/apex-cli
- **npm:** https://www.npmjs.com/package/apexcss-cli
- **License:** MIT

---

## Installation

```bash
npm install -g apexcss-cli
# or without installing globally:
npx apexcss <command>
```

Peer dependencies (must be installed in the user's project):
- `apexcss` ^0.4.3
- `sass` ≥ 1.100.0
- `vite` ≥ 8.0.0 (optional, for Vite-based projects)

Optional dependencies (installed separately for extra features):
- `lightningcss` — faster/better CSS minification when `--minify` is used
- `ws` — required for `apexcss serve` (WebSocket live reload)

---

## Commands

### `apexcss init [options]`
Scaffolds an `apex.config.js` in the current project. Detects framework automatically from `package.json`.

Options:
- `-f, --framework <name>` — override framework detection. Values: react, vue, angular, svelte, astro, next, nuxt, vanilla
- `--no-import` — skip injecting a CSS import into the framework entry file

Supported frameworks and their auto-import targets:

| Framework | Detection | Import target |
|-----------|-----------|---------------|
| Next.js | `next` dep | `globals.css` |
| React | `react` dep | `main.tsx` / `index.tsx` |
| Vue | `vue` dep | `main.ts` / `main.js` |
| Angular | `@angular/core` dep | `styles.css` |
| Svelte | `svelte` dep | `main.ts` / `App.svelte` |
| Astro | `astro` dep | `Layout.astro` |
| Nuxt | `nuxt` dep | `nuxt.config.ts` instructions |
| Vanilla | fallback | `main.js` / `main.ts` |

---

### `apexcss build [options]`
Compiles SCSS from the ApexCSS source (in `node_modules/apexcss/src`) using the current `apex.config.js`. Results are cached by content hash.

Options:
- `-l, --layer <layers>` — comma-separated layers to build. Default: `all`. Values: base, utilities, themes, all
- `--format <format>` — output format. Default: `css`. Values: css, scss, both
- `--minify` — minify output. Uses LightningCSS if installed, falls back to Sass compressed
- `--sourcemap` — emit `.css.map` files
- `--no-cache` — skip cache and force recompilation

Output files (default dir: `node_modules/apexcss/dist`):
- `base.css` — reset + base element styles
- `utilities.css` — utility classes (flex, spacing, colours, etc.)
- `themes.css` — design token overrides
- `apex.css` — complete combined output (only when all layers are built)

Cache: `.apexcss-cache/index.json` — keyed by SHA-256 of config + SCSS sources + build options. Invalidated automatically when any source file changes.

---

### `apexcss watch`
Watches `apex.config.js` for changes and runs a full rebuild on each save.

Inherits global options: `--config`, `--output`, `--minify`, `--sourcemap`.

---

### `apexcss doctor`
Checks the environment and reports:
- Node.js version (must be ≥ 22)
- Package manager presence (npm, pnpm, yarn)
- ApexCSS installation in node_modules
- `apex.config.js` presence
- Detected framework
- Detected build tool

---

### `apexcss purge [options]`
Two-phase optimisation:
1. Scans source files → extracts all CSS class names used
2. Disables ApexCSS feature modules not referenced by any class
3. Optionally tree-shakes the compiled CSS at the rule level (`--prune-css`)

Options:
- `--src <dirs>` — comma-separated directories to scan. Default: auto-detected from framework
- `--exclude <dirs>` — comma-separated directories to skip
- `--dry-run` — show proposed changes without writing anything
- `-y, --yes` — apply without prompting for confirmation
- `--backup` — write `apex.config.js.<timestamp>.backup` before modifying
- `--verbose-stats` — print per-class statistics (modifier counts, responsive variants, etc.)
- `--report <path>` — write full JSON analysis to a file
- `--prune-css` — after scanning, tree-shake compiled CSS files to remove unused rules
- `--css-dir <dir>` — directory containing compiled CSS to prune. Default: `node_modules/apexcss/dist`
- `--css-out <dir>` — output directory for pruned CSS. Default: same as `--css-dir` (overwrites in place)

Scanned file extensions: `.html`, `.htm`, `.jsx`, `.tsx`, `.ts`, `.vue`, `.svelte`, `.astro`, `.js`, `.mjs`, `.cjs`, `.mts`, `.cts`

Ignored directories: `node_modules`, `.git`, `dist`, `build`, `out`, `.next`, `.nuxt`, `coverage`, `public`, `static`, `assets`

Class extraction handles: HTML `class=`, JSX `className=`, Vue `:class=` (object + array syntax), Svelte `class:`, Alpine.js, template literals, `clsx`/`cn`/`classnames`/`cva`/`twMerge` utility calls.

---

### `apexcss analyze [options]`
Reads compiled CSS files and reports stats.

Options:
- `--output-dir <dir>` — directory to read CSS from. Default: `node_modules/apexcss/dist`
- `--json` — output results as JSON instead of a table
- `--report <path>` — save JSON report to a file

Output columns: file, size, gzip size, rules, selectors, declarations, media queries.
Also reports top 10 CSS property names by frequency.

JSON schema:
```json
{
  "timestamp": "ISO8601",
  "outputDir": "string",
  "totalSize": "number (bytes)",
  "totalGzipSize": "number (bytes)",
  "totalRules": "number",
  "files": [
    {
      "file": "string",
      "size": "number",
      "gzipSize": "number",
      "rules": "number",
      "selectors": "number",
      "declarations": "number",
      "mediaQueries": "number",
      "layers": "number"
    }
  ],
  "topProperties": [{ "property": "string", "count": "number" }]
}
```

---

### `apexcss migrate [options]`
Applies versioned transformation rules to `apex.config.js` to upgrade it between ApexCSS config format versions.

Options:
- `--from <version>` — source version. Default: `0.1.x`
- `--to <version>` — target version. Default: `0.3.0`
- `--dry-run` — show diff without writing
- `-y, --yes` — apply without confirmation

Shows a line-level diff before applying. Creates a backup automatically.

Built-in migrations:
- `0.1.x → 0.2.0`: renames `colors` → `palette`, wraps loose feature flags under `features` key
- `0.2.x → 0.3.0`: moves `palette` inside `features.palette`, renames `breakpoints` → `screens`

---

### `apexcss completion <shell>`
Prints a shell completion script to stdout.

Values: `bash`, `zsh`, `fish`

Install example:
```bash
apexcss completion bash > /usr/share/bash-completion/completions/apexcss
apexcss completion zsh  > /usr/local/share/zsh/site-functions/_apexcss
```

---

## Global Options

Available on all commands:

| Flag | Description | Default |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `-v, --version` | Print version and exit | — |
| `-c, --config <path>` | Path to config file | `./apex.config.js` |
| `-o, --output <dir>` | CSS output directory | `node_modules/apexcss/dist` |
| `--minify` | Minify CSS output | `false` |
| `--sourcemap` | Emit source maps | `false` |
| `-V, --verbose` | Verbose log output | `false` |
| `-q, --quiet` | Suppress all non-error output | `false` |
| `-h, --help` | Show help | — |

---

## Configuration File (`apex.config.js`)

```js
export default {
  features: {
    // Layout
    display: true,
    flexbox: true,
    grid: true,
    positioning: true,
    visibility: true,
    overflow: true,
    zIndex: true,
    // Spacing
    spacing: true,
    sizing: true,
    // Typography
    typography: true,
    typographyExtended: false,
    fontFamily: true,
    // Visual
    colors: true,
    backgrounds: true,
    borders: true,
    shadows: true,
    opacity: true,
    // Motion
    transitions: true,
    animations: true,
    transforms: true,
    transforms3d: false,
    // Misc
    filters: false,
    cursor: true,
    userSelect: true,
    pointerEvents: true,
    accessibility: true,
    tables: false,
    forms: false,
    print: false,
  },

  breakpoints: {
    sm: '320px',
    md: '768px',
    lg: '1024px',
    xl: '1280px',
  },

  colors: {
    primary: {
      hue: 250,
      chroma: 0.2,
      lightnessScale: {
        50: 96, 100: 90, 200: 85, 300: 78, 400: 70,
        500: 65, 600: 55, 700: 45, 800: 35, 900: 25, 950: 18
      }
    }
  },

  spacing: {
    '0': '0px', '1': '0.25rem', '2': '0.5rem',
    '4': '1rem', '8': '2rem', '16': '4rem'
  }
};
```

---

## Log Levels

The logger (`cli/utils/logger.ts`) supports these levels in ascending verbosity:

| Level | When active | Triggered by |
|-------|-------------|--------------|
| `silent` | Never | — |
| `error` | Always unless silent | — |
| `warn` | Default and above | — |
| `info` | Default | — |
| `verbose` | `--verbose` flag | `-V` |
| `debug` | `DEBUG` env var or debug level | `DEBUG=1` |

`--quiet` sets level to `error`. `--verbose` sets level to `verbose`.

---

## Exit Codes

- `0` — success
- `1` — any error (missing config, build failure, unknown command, etc.)

---

## Cache Location

`.apexcss-cache/index.json` in the project root. Add to `.gitignore`. Safe to delete — the next build recreates it.

---

## Feature → CSS Class Prefix Mapping (subset)

| Feature key | Example class prefixes |
|-------------|----------------------|
| `display` | `block`, `inline`, `flex`, `grid`, `hidden` |
| `flexbox` | `flex-`, `justify-`, `items-`, `self-`, `grow`, `shrink` |
| `grid` | `grid-cols-`, `col-`, `row-`, `gap-` |
| `spacing` | `p-`, `m-`, `px-`, `py-`, `mx-`, `my-`, `pt-`, `pb-` |
| `typography` | `text-`, `font-`, `leading-`, `tracking-`, `uppercase`, `lowercase` |
| `colors` | `text-{color}-{shade}`, `bg-{color}-{shade}`, `border-{color}-{shade}` |
| `borders` | `border`, `border-`, `rounded-` |
| `shadows` | `shadow-` |
| `transitions` | `transition`, `duration-`, `ease-`, `delay-` |
| `transforms` | `scale-`, `rotate-`, `translate-` |
| `animations` | `animate-` |
| `opacity` | `opacity-` |
| `positioning` | `static`, `relative`, `absolute`, `fixed`, `sticky`, `top-`, `inset-` |

Full mappings: `cli/utils/feature-mapper.ts`
